dataset.name: EID Quick Reference - Azure AD / Entra ID (Device-Side)

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: Primary Refresh Token operation failed
entries.summary: The device failed to acquire or refresh its Primary Refresh Token from Azure AD / Entra ID.
entries.details: Generated when the Microsoft-Windows-AAD (Azure AD/Entra ID) device authentication component fails to acquire or refresh the Primary Refresh Token (PRT). The PRT is the core credential for device-based SSO in Entra ID and hybrid-joined environments — it is issued to the device based on its Entra ID device registration certificate and is used to silently obtain access tokens for cloud resources. Repeated PRT failures can indicate: (1) the device certificate used for PRT issuance is invalid, expired, or revoked (e.g., after device compliance violation or Intune non-compliance), (2) the device has been disabled in Entra ID, (3) a Conditional Access Policy is blocking device authentication, or (4) the device certificate has been corrupted or deleted. In Pass-the-PRT attack scenarios, an adversary who has extracted the PRT from the device causes the legitimate device to experience PRT failure because the token was used from an unexpected location — the cloud side invalidates the PRT on anomaly detection.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token

investigationPivots: Repeated PRT failures on a device that was previously successfully acquiring PRTs may indicate the device certificate was tampered with or the device was disabled in Entra ID
Correlate with Entra ID sign-in logs for the same user — if there are successful sign-ins from a different device or location at the time of device PRT failure, suspect PRT theft
Error code 0xCAA50021 (device not compliant) indicates Conditional Access blocked the PRT; check device compliance status in Intune
Error codes indicating certificate issues (0xCAA5001C, 0xCAA90031) warrant examination of the device's Entra ID registration certificate in certlm.msc
commonFalsePositives: Device out of Intune compliance triggering CA policy that blocks PRT issuance
Hybrid-join misconfiguration preventing PRT acquisition on newly domain-joined devices
Network connectivity issues preventing the device from reaching Azure AD endpoints (login.microsoftonline.com)

keyFields.name: Error Code
keyFields.description: Hex error code indicating why the PRT operation failed; 0xCAA50021 = not compliant, 0xCAA5001C = certificate invalid

keyFields.name: User
keyFields.description: UPN of the user whose PRT operation failed

keyFields.name: OperationType
keyFields.description: Whether this was an acquire or refresh operation

entries.title: Primary Refresh Token refreshed successfully
entries.summary: The device successfully refreshed its Primary Refresh Token from Azure AD / Entra ID.
entries.details: Generated when the device successfully acquires or refreshes the Primary Refresh Token (PRT) from Entra ID. In normal operation on Entra ID-joined or hybrid-joined devices, this event fires periodically as the PRT is refreshed (the PRT has a 14-day validity but is refreshed on each authentication). This is a baseline event used to establish normal PRT refresh patterns per device.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token

investigationPivots: PRT refreshed for a privileged account (Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin UPN) on an unmanaged or non-compliant device warrants investigation
Correlate with Entra ID sign-in logs — if the PRT was refreshed on the device but cloud sign-ins appear from a different IP/device simultaneously, suspect Pass-the-PRT
Unexpected PRT refresh after a period of device inactivity (e.g., device was thought to be decommissioned) indicates the device is still active and possibly under attacker control
PRT refresh at unusual hours or for a privileged account on an unexpected device is anomalous
commonFalsePositives: Normal periodic PRT refresh on active Entra ID-joined devices (occurs with each authentication)
PRT refresh after resume from sleep or hibernate on laptops
Device clock drift causing token expiry and immediate re-refresh

keyFields.name: User
keyFields.description: UPN of the user whose PRT was refreshed

entries.title: SSO token silently issued for cloud resource
entries.summary: The WAM broker silently issued an access token for a cloud resource using the device PRT.
entries.details: Generated when the Web Account Manager (WAM) broker silently obtains an access token for a cloud resource using the device's Primary Refresh Token, without requiring user interaction. This event documents silent SSO — the mechanism by which Windows silently authenticates users to cloud resources (Microsoft 365, Azure management, third-party SaaS apps) using the PRT.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token

investigationPivots: Token issued silently for Azure management plane (management.azure.com) or Microsoft Graph from a standard user device indicates the user's account or a local application is accessing admin-tier cloud resources
Correlate with Sysmon EID 1 or Security EID 4688 on the same device to identify which process triggered the WAM broker request
Multiple silent tokens issued for different resources in rapid succession indicate a local application enumerating cloud resource access
commonFalsePositives: Microsoft 365 applications (Outlook, Teams, SharePoint) silently refreshing tokens for cloud resources is normal and generates high volume
Security and compliance agents (MCAS, Defender for Endpoint) silently authenticating to management APIs
Intune/MDM clients silently authenticating to management endpoints

keyFields.name: Resource
keyFields.description: Cloud resource or API for which the token was issued

keyFields.name: User
keyFields.description: UPN of the user for whom the token was issued

keyFields.name: TokenType
keyFields.description: Type of token issued (access token, refresh token)

entries.title: SSO to cloud resource failed
entries.summary: The WAM broker failed to obtain a token for a cloud resource — denied by Entra ID or Conditional Access.
entries.details: Generated when the Web Account Manager (WAM) broker fails to silently obtain an access token for a cloud resource. The failure may be due to Conditional Access Policy (CAP) enforcement, device compliance requirements, MFA requirements, or token scope restrictions. EID 6145 for resources that the user was previously able to access silently may indicate a new Conditional Access Policy, a device compliance status change, PRT revocation due to risk detection (Entra ID Identity Protection), or an added MFA requirement.

investigationPivots: Sudden 6145 failures after a period of successful 6144 SSO events may indicate Entra ID Identity Protection flagged the account as risky — check Entra ID sign-in risk logs
Correlate with Intune device compliance status — non-compliant device triggering CA policy is the most common legitimate cause
Error codes indicating MFA required (AADSTS50076) when the user did not recently change their sign-in risk may indicate a new CA policy was applied to the resource
A sudden pattern of 6145 events correlated with EID 1098 (PRT failure) may indicate that Entra ID detected anomalous behavior (risk-based CA trigger) and is blocking the device
commonFalsePositives: Device compliance state changes (Intune check-in after non-compliance period)
Network connectivity issues preventing WAM from reaching Entra ID endpoints
New Conditional Access policies applied during an IT change window

keyFields.name: Resource
keyFields.description: Target cloud resource that was denied

keyFields.name: Error Code
keyFields.description: Entra ID error code; AADSTS50076 = MFA required, AADSTS53003 = blocked by CA policy

keyFields.name: User
keyFields.description: UPN of the user whose SSO failed

entries.title: WAM brokered authentication request
entries.summary: An application on this device requested authentication via the Web Account Manager (WAM) broker.
entries.details: Generated when an application invokes the Windows Web Account Manager (WAM) broker to request authentication tokens from Entra ID. WAM is the standard Windows authentication broker for modern applications — it intermediates between the application and Entra ID, using the device PRT to silently issue tokens. This event documents which applications on the device are requesting cloud authentication, providing a map of application-to-resource access.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token

investigationPivots: Application ID not matching known enterprise applications or Microsoft first-party apps (Office, Teams, etc.) requesting WAM tokens is anomalous
PowerShell, wscript, or cmd process requesting WAM brokered auth for management-plane resources indicates malicious script abusing WAM for token theft
Correlate with Sysmon EID 1 (process creation) to identify the parent process chain for the WAM request — legitimate apps are typically signed, known binaries
An attacker with code execution on the device can invoke WAM to silently obtain tokens for any resource the user has access to, without prompting for credentials
commonFalsePositives: All modern Microsoft applications (Office 365, Teams, Edge, Outlook) use WAM for authentication — generates significant volume
Line-of-business applications integrated with Entra ID via MSAL making routine token requests
Third-party applications using MSAL (Microsoft Authentication Library) for Entra ID integration

keyFields.name: Resource
keyFields.description: Cloud resource or API requested by the application

keyFields.name: User
keyFields.description: UPN of the user on whose behalf the token was requested
